claim: There is exactly 1 hard dependency across all 7 plans: agent-env (all 5 phases) must complete before SWS Phase 1
evidence: Memory note explicitly states 'All 5 phases must complete before SWS Phase 1.' STEP_3_14_COVERAGE_AUDIT.md confirms agent-env subsumes SWS Step 3.14. PLAN-v3.md Step 13 relies on improved agent definitions from agent-env Phase 4.

claim: SWS dominates total effort at 80-130+ sessions, constituting ~85% of total effort across all 7 plans
evidence: S-07 effort table sums to 80-130 sessions. User confirmed this is a floor, not ceiling (scope grows during execution). All 6 non-SWS plans combined require ~13-19 sessions.

claim: CANON is built WITHIN SWS Step 1, not an external dependency. SWS has no external hard blocks except agent-env completion.
evidence: SWS PLAN.md defines CANON as Step 1 (sub-steps 1a-1i). DIAGNOSIS.md framing of 'SWS gated on CANON framework' is misleading. Verified in S-07 findings line 177-179.

claim: 5 of 7 plans have zero hard dependencies on each other. Custom-statusline is completely independent.
evidence: S-09 dependency graph verified: no cycles, 1 hard dependency (AE->SWS), 5 soft dependencies pointing toward SWS. Custom-statusline has zero overlap files with most plans.

claim: .husky/pre-commit is the most contested shared resource, touched by 4 of 7 plans (PS, PR, AE, SWS)
evidence: S-08 file overlap matrix verified against filesystem. File exists at 34KB. PS adds Fix: command, PR adds EXIT trap, AE adds agent triggers, SWS adds CANON gates. All verified in respective plan inventories.

claim: session-start.js is a 3-way conflict: CLI (tool detection), PS (7 violation fixes), AE (token monitoring). 37KB, 1077 lines.
evidence: File size verified via filesystem. Three plans' modifications confirmed in S-03 Step 18, S-04 Step 1, S-06 Step 5.4. All three make substantive changes.

claim: Only 2 of 32 S0 critical debt items are resolved by any of the 7 plans. Both are resolved by propagation-research.
evidence: DEBT-2121 (command injection) resolved by propagation Step 9. DEBT-9295 (CI non-blocking) resolved by propagation Step 2. All 15 spot-checked DEBT IDs verified against MASTER_DEBT.jsonl.

claim: Propagation is the only S0-resolver and has the highest debt-reduction ROI of any plan
evidence: Resolves 2 S0 items and 3-5 S1 items in ~17 hours. No other plan resolves any S0 items. SWS has higher total S1 impact but at 80-130 sessions of effort.

claim: Repo-cleanup is zero-risk and benefits 5 other plans. Should execute first (Wave 0).
evidence: All changes verified against filesystem. 2 missing files already documented. Easy rollback (2 git commits). DIAGNOSIS.md confirms 'should go first.' S-01 verified post-conditions benefit SWS, CLI, PS, AE, PR.

claim: SWS decomposes into 7 chunks with clean interleaving points at checkpoint boundaries
evidence: S-16 decomposition verified: chunk step ranges cover all 21 steps, dependency graph matches plan D63/D67, effort estimates are direct sums of S-07 per-step values. Born-compliant gates activate progressively.

claim: 16 redundancies identified across plans, but actual dedup savings are only ~1 hour. Most are coordination needs, not duplicated work.
evidence: S-10 identified 16 redundancies (R-01 through R-16). All verified in CL. Most involve different plans modifying the same file for different purposes. Coordination cost, not work elimination.

claim: 14 synergies identified. Optimal ordering saves ~5-9 SWS sessions (~7% total effort) through synergy exploitation.
evidence: S-10 synergy table with 14 entries (SY-01 through SY-14). Each verified in CL. SY-02 (PS before SWS saves 1-2 sessions) and SY-09 (PR scripts before SWS saves 1-2 sessions) are the largest.

claim: The scheduling problem is a 'star graph with one heavy leaf' -- trivially solved. Formal algorithms (Coffman-Graham, RCPSP, Hu's) are overkill.
evidence: 7 tasks, 1 hard dependency, 100:1 duration ratio, 1 processor (solo dev). Algorithms designed for 30-120+ activities with comparable durations. Manual inspection resolves shared resource conflicts optimally for 5 resources.

claim: Agent-env Phase 4 is the highest-risk remaining phase due to interactive per-agent decisions and scope creep potential. Mean audit score was 51/100 (grade F).
evidence: S-06 Key Finding #5 rates Phase 4 as highest-risk. Phase 3 audit found mean score 51/100. Plan mitigates with 'done when' criteria but Phase 4 involves 36+ agents with interactive user decisions.

claim: planning-32326 branch should merge to main before any plan execution begins
evidence: S-15 identifies that plan branches created from main without orchestration research artifacts will lack context. Orchestration artifacts should be on main so all plan branches inherit them.

claim: 8 PRs recommended: one per plan, propagation split W1/W2-4. SWS uses per-checkpoint branches.
evidence: S-15 branch strategy: each plan gets own branch for isolation. Propagation split reduces risk exposure. SWS checkpoint-merge strategy limits blast radius. All file conflicts resolved in merge order.

claim: Pre-commit hook is the single most dangerous shared resource -- broken pre-commit blocks ALL git commits across the project
evidence: S-17 identifies pre-commit as CRITICAL impact. 4 plans modify it. Section-based ownership and post-modification testing recommended but not yet formalized.

claim: Passive-surfacing must complete session-start.js changes before CLI modifies the file. PS first, CLI second, AE last.
evidence: S-08 CONFLICT-H1. PS fixes 7 violations (existing code). CLI adds new tool detection block. AE adds monitoring. Running CLI first means building on broken surfacing. S-12 cascade analysis confirms.

claim: PS ecosystem audit skill categories must be added before PR shared-lib extraction (4 overlapping files)
evidence: S-10 R-08 and S-08 CONFLICT-M3. PS adds additive content (new category text). PR performs structural refactoring. If PR runs first, PS target locations change.

claim: 3 active S0 issues remain unaddressed by any plan: hard-coded password patterns (likely false positives), OS command execution in check-review-needed.js, fast-xml-parser DoS vulnerability
evidence: S-11 unaddressed S0 analysis. DEBT-4399/4400/11124 (hard-coded passwords), DEBT-4403/11126 (OS command), DEBT-7544/11283 (fast-xml-parser). No plan targets these files or issues.

claim: Repo-cleanup plan has a wrong file path: references scripts/config/rotation-policy.json but actual file is config/rotation-policy.json
evidence: S-01 verified via filesystem and rotate-jsonl.js script (line 231 confirms path.resolve(projectRoot, 'config', 'rotation-policy.json')). Plan line 144 has incorrect path.

claim: Custom-statusline is the most isolated plan. tools/statusline/ is entirely new. Only overlaps on settings.json and .gitignore.
evidence: S-02 and S-08 verify: 11 new files in new directory. Only 2 existing files modified. Zero file overlap with RC, PS, PR. Minimal overlap with CLI (settings.json, different keys).

claim: Agent-env has the widest modification footprint: 50+ files across agents, skills, hooks, scripts, and config
evidence: S-06: Phase 4 modifies up to 37 agent files. Phase 5 modifies CLAUDE.md, 4+ skills, pre-commit, pre-push, hook config, invocation scripts, statusline, session-end, alerts. Total 50+.

claim: Total elapsed time for all 7 plans: 86-139+ sessions (43-70+ working days at 2 sessions/day)
evidence: S-14 wave duration summary: Wave 0 (1) + Wave 1 (5-8) + Wave 2 (6-10) + Wave 3 (74-120) = 86-139. User confirmed SWS is a floor, so pessimistic may exceed 139.

claim: Propagation Wave 2 (mass refactoring of 100+ files) is the highest-risk non-SWS work item
evidence: S-05 Steps 6-7 modify 55+34 files. S-17 rates propagation MEDIUM-HIGH overall with Step 8 (186 files) as 'single highest-risk step across all 7 plans.' Per-wave commits needed for rollback.

claim: SWS Checkpoint #1 is the highest-risk gate in the entire project. If CANON design is wrong, all 20 downstream steps are wrong.
evidence: S-07 and S-17: CP#1 validates CANON end-to-end. D68 skip-and-return does NOT apply to CP#1. If CANON design fails, correcting costs 6-10 sessions. Foundation (Steps 1-4) risk rated HIGH.

claim: Passive-surfacing's 33 violations exist OUTSIDE the MASTER_DEBT tracking system, revealing a TDMS coverage gap
evidence: S-11: guardrail #6 violations tracked in plan research, not MASTER_DEBT.jsonl. MASTER_DEBT has no 'passive-surfacing' or 'fire-and-forget' category. 33 violations invisible to debt analytics.

claim: The Meta Pipeline sequence (T&I -> CQ -> DE -> SWS) status is ambiguous -- either still enforced (blocking SWS) or superseded by SWS internal sequencing
evidence: S-13: ROADMAP declares strict P0 sequential pipeline. DIAGNOSIS lists only SWS among 7 plans. SWS PLAN says CANON has zero external deps. Meta Pipeline sub-plans exist as subdirectories. Genuine ambiguity.

claim: SWS scope grows during execution -- the 80-130 session estimate is a floor, not a ceiling
evidence: User constraint document explicitly states SWS is a 'plan for making plans' whose scope increases as each ecosystem standardization discovers new work.

claim: Even fully sequential execution of non-SWS plans adds only 7-11 sessions to total -- parallelism benefit is real but modest
evidence: S-14 sensitivity analysis: aggressive interleaving (5-6 sessions Wave 1) vs fully sequential (13-19 sessions) = 7-11 session difference. Within SWS estimation uncertainty range.

claim: Rolling wave planning is the most valuable scheduling concept: plan Wave 0-1 in detail, measure SWS velocity for 10 sessions, then re-estimate
evidence: S-14: SWS has 50-session uncertainty range. No scheduling algorithm can reduce this -- only execution experience narrows it. Rolling wave planning acknowledged as best-fit execution strategy.

claim: Time-box agent-env Phase 4 to 2 sessions maximum. Remaining agents go to SWS Step 13 backlog.
evidence: S-14 and S-17 recommend time-boxing to protect critical path. Phase 4 involves interactive per-agent decisions with scope creep risk. Priority agents (code-reviewer, security-auditor, GSD) provide 80% of value.

claim: Propagation plan has file name mismatch: Step 14 references generate-doc-index.js but actual file is generate-documentation-index.js
evidence: S-05 filesystem verification. package.json script docs:index runs node scripts/generate-documentation-index.js. Plan uses shorthand name.

claim: cli-tools has no uninstall script -- rollback of external changes (14+ binaries, .bashrc, .gitconfig) requires manual removal
evidence: S-17 gap analysis. Plan creates install scripts but no corresponding uninstall capability. Repo changes are git-revertible but external changes are not tracked.
